change_comments.message:
all looks good.
if u can write a simple test to prove that the error code is changed from 500 to 400 now.

test a case for invalid JSON string at the controller layer is not applicable because controller just delegates the input to service layer and only then the data is validated. The only input validated at controller layer is the timestamp.

makes sense now.
We might be able to get the correct response code when using POSTMAN and forcefully provide Accept header as application/json , BUT when using swagger , in case of any validation issue it will result in 5XX response code instead of 4XX.

This fix will streamline our openapi specs as well.

inline_comments.message:
just thinking now.
if you change the content to application/json now , does it need to have a relevant schema for the same and not just string ??

no a schema is not required as this handles the response in case of a write operation. So, when we post some data to be written to database usually nothing is returned in response body.
As per my understanding, since Created response was set to "text/plain" while other scenarios like BadRequest, Unauthorized, etc were set to "application/json", it was causing a conflict as to what the proper response format should be and hence it fell back to response code 500 as a default.
And you can see that for the delta feature where we have a POST operation the response used is OK instead of Created because there, we return the delta report and OK response uses "application/json" which is consistent with other responses.

the exception was thrown in the logs is as follows:
